steps.title: Identify the structure of the model
steps.content: $N(d) = 115 \times (0.90)^d$ is exponential with base $0.90$. A base less than $1$ means each unit increase in $d$ multiplies $N$ by $0.90$, a $10\%$ decrease.

steps.title: Translate the input units
steps.content: The variable $d$ is measured in hundreds of meters, so increasing $d$ by $1$ corresponds to a real-world depth increase of $100$ meters.

steps.title: Combine the two facts
steps.content: Each $100$ m increase in depth multiplies the species count by $0.90$, i.e. a $10\%$ decrease.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For every increase in depth by 1 meter, the number of brachiopod species decreases by 115.’: a $1$ m increase is not one unit of $d$, and $115$ is the initial value, not a per-step decrease.
  • Choice B — ‘For every increase in depth by 1 meter, the number of brachiopod species decreases by 10%.’: a $1$ m increase is only $0.01$ units of $d$, so the $10\%$ figure does not apply per meter.
  • Choice C — ‘For every increase in depth by 100 meters, the number of brachiopod species decreases by 115.’: $100$ m is the right step, but $115$ is the starting value, not the amount lost.
  • Choice D — ‘For every increase in depth by 100 meters, the number of brachiopod species decreases by 10%.’: $100$ m equals one unit of $d$, and one unit multiplies $N$ by $0.90$, a $10\%$ decrease.
That confirms D is correct.
